summary: Supervising and overseeing the direction of the project, ensuring that the client's specifications and requirements are met, reviewing progress and liaising with quantity surveyors to monitor costs
raw_text:
CONSTRUCTION SITE MANAGER
Summary
supervising and overseeing the direction of the project (or a package), ensuring that the client's specifications and requirements are met, reviewing
progress and liaising with quantity surveyors to monitor costs
liaising with the client, other construction professionals and, sometimes, members of the public
coordinating and supervising construction workers
selecting tools and materials
making safety inspections and ensuring construction and site safety
checking and preparing site reports, designs and drawings
maintaining quality control procedures
finding ways to prevent problems and to solve any that crop up
assessing and minimising risk
writing reports and keeping on top of paperwork
helping to negotiating contracts and securing permits and licences
As construction managers work on site wherever that site is, be prepared to relocate or face a longer commute as and when required.
Skills
Good communication skills Networking ability
Problem solving skills Lead development
Decision-making ability Product expertise
Commercial awareness Crisis communications
Ability to motivate other Marketing
Teamworking skills Blueprint reading
Good knowledge of building methods and regulations Regulation and compliance
Customer service Excellent driving record
Account management Detail-oriented
Business-to-business background Good mechanical aptitude
Capital planning Production line/assembly line forklift experience
Staff training Good verbal/written communication skills
Contract negotiation Establishing goals and setting priorities
